retrieved_docs.title: Sonography 3rd Trimester and Placenta Assessment, Protocols, and Interpretation -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: There are few contraindications for the use of transabdominal ultrasound during the third trimester of pregnancy. The only universally accepted absolute contraindication is patient refusal, following the principle of patient autonomy.

retrieved_docs.title: Sonography 3rd Trimester and Placenta Assessment, Protocols, and Interpretation -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Traditionally, the third trimester is defined as the antenatal period between 28 and 42 weeks of gestation. There is currently no universal, standardized protocol for assessing the progression and status of the pregnancy during this period.

retrieved_docs.content: Fetal intrauterine growth restriction, with no abnormal Doppler, with the timing at 38 0/7 to 39 6/7 weeks of gestation

retrieved_docs.title: Sonography 3rd Trimester and Placenta Assessment, Protocols, and Interpretation -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: A particular patient population that is especially benefited from using ultrasound in the third trimester is the late presenter without prior prenatal care. Unfortunately, these patients are prone to all aforementioned disorders, and an ultrasonographic evaluation is of paramount importance to avoid, or at least plan for, significant morbidity and mortality causes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 1975
retrieved_docs.content: INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with الولادة القيصرية (cesarean delivery). The optional procedure إنتهاء الحمل - اختياري (termination of pregnancy – optional) is also clinically associated with الولادة القيصرية, indicating that decisions around pregnancy termination may influence the likelihood of a cesarean section. Additionally, الولادة القيصرية frequently co‑occurs with ربط البوق, suggesting that this specific practice or condition is commonly observed in the context of cesarean deliveries. Recognizing that preterm low‑birth‑weight infants, elective pregnancy termination, and ربط البوق are all linked to cesarean delivery helps clinicians anticipate related risks, plan appropriate obstetric interventions, and provide comprehensive counseling to patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2073
retrieved_docs.content: COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY encompasses several serious obstetric conditions, specifically hemorrhage during pregnancy, abruptio placenta, and placenta previa. These three entities are classified as hemorrhagic complications of pregnancy, representing major sources of maternal morbidity and mortality. Recognizing that hemorrhage during pregnancy, abruptio placenta, and placenta previa fall under the broader category of pregnancy‑related complications highlights their interrelated nature and underscores the importance of prompt diagnosis and coordinated management in maternal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2388
retrieved_docs.content: Hemorrhage during pregnancy, including the specific conditions abruptio placenta and placenta previa, are classified together as types of obstetric hemorrhage. This classification groups these distinct pregnancy‑related bleeding disorders, emphasizing their shared relevance to maternal health and guiding clinical assessment and management of hemorrhagic complications in pregnant patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 2 -- Community 570
retrieved_docs.content: preterm birth, chorioamnionitis), maternal factors (cervical pathology, ectopy), delivery modes (cesarean, forceps, vacuum‑assisted, extraction), fetal findings (missed abortion, low birth weight), and pregnancy classifications (selective, optional termination), providing a comprehensive framework for perinatal screening, clinical decision‑making, and management of maternal‑fetal health.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2849
retrieved_docs.content: Poor reproductive history and elective termination of pregnancy are clinically associated with preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان). This relationship indicates that women who have experienced a suboptimal reproductive history or who have undergone an elective termination of pregnancy may be at increased risk for developing preterm labor. Recognizing these associations can help clinicians identify patients who require closer monitoring and targeted interventions to prevent or manage preterm labor, thereby improving maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 3 -- Community 1799
retrieved_docs.content: Elective termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري) and forceps delivery (الولادة بالملقط) are clinically associated obstetric interventions. Both procedures are employed within the spectrum of pregnancy management: elective termination represents a planned ending of gestation, while forceps delivery is a method of assisted vaginal birth used when specific maternal or fetal conditions warrant intervention. Their clinical association highlights the range of obstetric care options available to address diverse maternal‑fetal indications, and recognizing this relationship assists clinicians in selecting the most appropriate intervention to optimize outcomes for both mother and newborn.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2304
retrieved_docs.content: Early, first or unspecified trimester hemorrhage is classified as spotting complicating pregnancy, unspecified trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1494
retrieved_docs.content: A gestational age of 29‑30 weeks completed is associated with a liveborn infant, indicating that pregnancies reaching this stage can result in a live birth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2324
retrieved_docs.content: Induction by cervical dilation and other procedures to assist delivery are closely related interventions used to facilitate childbirth, and they may be considered synonymous in clinical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 1 -- Community 129
retrieved_docs.content: outcome category ولادة جنين ميت (delivery of a dead fetus), indicating that the combined risks of surgical termination, severe hemorrhage, placental abruption, and placenta previa contribute to the possibility of stillbirth. Together, these entities and relationships illustrate how specific amniotic cavity problems, early membrane rupture, cesarean delivery, missed abortion, anesthesia‑related cardiac and central nervous system complications, fetal disorders, and termination‑related hemorrhagic conditions are interlinked with pregnancy complications and their late effects, ultimately influencing the risk of delivering a non‑viable infant and underscoring the importance of careful obstetric monitoring and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 1 -- Community 200
retrieved_docs.content: منزاحة من دون نزف (marginal placenta without bleeding), placental necrosis (نخر المشيمة), maternal and fetal hemorrhage (نزف الأم والجنين), and uterine tumor (ورم الرحم) are frequently encountered. Recognizing these interconnections is essential for comprehensive obstetric risk assessment, diagnosis, and management.
